mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This mechanism is considered always-on, reflecting the underlying health of the economy.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS 3-month average growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative (average return < 0) over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. This indicates a shift into an economic contraction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate is also high, it indicates persistent monetary tightening or a stagflationary environment. This typically compresses corporate margins and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL 12-month growth exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive on average (mean return > 0) over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A robust housing market, indicated by strong housing starts, reflects healthy consumer and business confidence, which stimulates broader economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. This mechanism is particularly potent during economic expansions.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows sustained 3-month average growth above 5.0% but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such periods over a 5-year test, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth signifies healthy consumer demand and spending, which directly contributes to corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one trading week. This reflects immediate consumer sentiment.
failure_condition: If RSAFS 1-month growth exceeds 0.3% but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative on average over any 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing and overall economic activity, suggesting reduced corporate output and earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This reflects a contraction in the supply side.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a 3-month average decline greater than 0.5% but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year test, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, stimulates investment, and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0% for a sustained period (e.g., 6 consecutive months) but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative on average, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in employment growth and retail sales indicates a robust and expanding economy driven by strong consumer fundamentals. This provides a solid foundation for corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon of one trading week.
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS and RSAFS show positive 1-month growth above their respective thresholds, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 30% of such instances over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.
